title: T-011 — Outbox publisher: quality.hold.* events (12-REP/05-WH consumer)
prompt:
# T-011 — Outbox publisher: quality.hold.* events

PRD: 09-QUALITY-PRD.md §12.1 outbound events table, ADR-008 outbox pattern
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Wire the outbox publisher worker so quality.hold.created and quality.hold.released events written by T-006/T-007 are picked up and dispatched to consumer endpoints (05-WH, 12-REP) with retry, idempotency, and DLQ.

## Implementation contract
1. `apps/web/src/server/outbox/qualityHoldPublisher.ts` extends the shared outbox poller registry (existing module) with handler `'quality.hold.created'` and `'quality.hold.released'` mapping to deliver functions per consumer.
2. Idempotency: outbox row UUIDv7 used as request_id header; consumers ignore replays with same id.
3. Retry: exponential backoff (1s, 5s, 25s, 125s, 600s), then DLQ insert into outbox_dead_letter with original payload.
4. Metric increment `quality_hold_outbox_published_total{event}` and `..._failed_total{event,reason}`.

## Files
- apps/web/src/server/outbox/qualityHoldPublisher.ts [create]
- apps/web/src/server/outbox/__tests__/qualityHoldPublisher.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given an outbox row with event='quality.hold.created' and 2 fake consumers wired, when the publisher polls, then both consumers receive the payload with the same request_id header equal to outbox.id
2. Given a consumer that fails 5 times, when retry budget exceeded, then the row is moved to outbox_dead_letter with original payload preserved and the metric ..._failed_total{reason='retry_exhausted'} increments
3. Given two pollers running concurrently picking the same row, when both attempt to publish, then only one succeeds (advisory lock or claim-token enforces single-flight)

pipeline_inputs.description: Outbox publisher handlers for quality.hold.created and quality.hold.released with retry, idempotency, DLQ.
pipeline_inputs.details: Register handlers in shared outbox poller. Each handler dispatches the payload to known consumer fan-out endpoints (05-WH, 12-REP). Idempotency via outbox row UUIDv7 -> request_id header. Retry with exponential backoff capped at 5 attempts then DLQ. Single-flight via Postgres advisory lock keyed by outbox.id. Metrics emitted via existing prometheus client.
out_of_scope: Do not change shared outbox poller core (out of scope this task)
Do not implement consumer-side idempotency in 05-WH or 12-REP (their responsibility)
acceptance_criteria: Given an outbox row event='quality.hold.created' with 2 fake consumers, when the publisher polls, then both consumers receive the payload with request_id equal to outbox.id

Given a consumer that fails 5 times, when the retry budget is exhausted, then the row is moved to outbox_dead_letter with original payload preserved and metric ..._failed_total{reason='retry_exhausted'} increments by 1
Given two pollers concurrently claiming the same row, when both attempt to publish, then exactly one succeeds (advisory lock single-flight enforced)
test_strategy: RED: write apps/web/src/server/outbox/__tests__/qualityHoldPublisher.test.ts asserting fan-out, retry/DLQ, single-flight
risk_red_lines: Do not lose events on transient consumer failures (DLQ mandatory)
Do not allow concurrent double-publish (idempotency contract for downstream)
Do not silently drop payload fields (full event body preserved end-to-end)
